content_markdown:
The diagram shows a train travelling in a straight line at constant speed $v$, as it approaches the platform of a station.

The whistle of the engine is emitting a sound of constant frequency. Which of the following is **not** true for the sound of the whistle heard by an observer on the platform?

diagram_metadata.description: A train moving to the right with velocity v towards a platform where an observer is standing.

options.label: A
options.text: A sudden change in frequency of the sound as the train passes the observer.

options.label: B
options.text: A sound of constant frequency as the train approaches the observer.

options.label: C
options.text: A sound of increasing frequency as the train approaches the observer and of decreasing frequency after the train has passed the observer.

options.label: D
options.text: A sound of constant frequency after the train has passed the observer.

vibe_explanation: According to the Doppler effect formula for a moving source $f' = f \frac{v}{v \mp v_s}$, if the source speed $v_s$ is constant, the observed frequency $f'$ is constant (higher than the source frequency while approaching, lower while receding). Therefore, the frequency does not 'increase' as it approaches; it stays at a constant elevated value until the moment it passes the observer.
